title: Wizard async transfer trigger (CC3-898 / CC3-896)
description: Per-item trigger JSON written by Drupal to s3://{trigger-bucket}/transfer-actions/{request_id}-{item_id}-{operation}.json. Read by the ieee-rc-wizard-transfer Lambda, which streams the source bytes (Google Drive or URL) into S3 via multipart upload and POSTs an HMAC-signed callback to Drupal. This file MUST be byte-identical in Samson-Labs/ieee-cc and Samson-Labs/ieee-cc-python.

version.description: Schema version. Bump when introducing breaking changes.

source_type.description: google_drive uses drive_oauth_token_ref + Drive API; url is a plain HTTPS GET.

source_ref.description: Drive file ID (when source_type=google_drive) or absolute https:// URL (when source_type=url).

drive_oauth_token_ref.description: AWS Secrets Manager secret name holding the user's Drive OAuth access token. Required when source_type=google_drive. Naming convention: iplr/drive-tokens/{request_id}-{item_id}. Drupal mints the secret per dispatch (s3fs role needs CreateSecret/PutSecretValue/DeleteSecret on iplr/drive-tokens/*); Lambda only reads it.

dest_bucket.description: Destination S3 bucket. Lambda needs s3:PutObject/s3:AbortMultipartUpload on this bucket.

dest_key.description: Full destination S3 key including any OU prefix (e.g. ou-bps/pending/{filename}.mp4). Lambda does not transform this.

item_id.description: Drupal ProductLoadItem entity id; echoed back in the callback.

request_id.description: Drupal correlation id (a per-dispatch counter); echoed back in the callback. Used in CloudWatch logs.

operation.description: Discriminator for downstream handling on the Drupal side. Lambda treats both identically (raw byte transfer).

callback_url.description: Drupal endpoint Lambda POSTs the result to (e.g. https://cc-ieee.lndo.site/api/iplr/webhook/media-transfer).

callback_secret_ref.description: AWS Secrets Manager secret name holding the HMAC-SHA256 signing key (shared with the existing AI webhook secret today).
